data.title: Ross Stores gains after smashing comparable sales expectations on strong back-to-school demand
data.description:
Ross Stores (ROST) traded higher in postmarket trading on Thursday after the off-price retailer beat estimates on both lines of its Q3 earnings report and set favorable profit guidance.

Comparable sales rose 7% year-over-year during the quarter to smash the consensus expectation for a 3.3% gain. The retailer noted that it had an excellent back-to-school season with strong trends that continued through the balance of the quarter.

Operating margin for the quarter was 11.6% vs. 10.6% consensus.

"We enter the holiday season with strong momentum and are well-positioned to offer a compelling merchandise assortment across all our stores," stated CEO Jim Conroy. Guidance was set for Q4 comparable store sales to be up 3% to 4%, with earnings per share in the range of $1.77 to $1.85. Tariff-related costs are anticipated to be negligible in the fourth quarter after impacting full-year results by an estimated $0.16 per share.

Ross Stores (ROST) ended the quarter with 2,273 stores vs. 2,291 a year ago. Merchandise inventory was $3.13 billion vs. $2.86 billion a year ago.

Shares of Ross Stores (ROST) shot up 4.5% in postmarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: Google touts voluntary exit deals to UK employees - report
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) is offering voluntary exit packages to an unspecified number of employees in its UK offices, according to a report by Business Insider on Thursday.

"Earlier this year, some of our US teams introduced a voluntary exit programme with severance for U.S.-based Googlers, and we are now also offering the programme in the UK to support our important work ahead," a Google spokesperson told Business Insider.

"We are committed to investment in the UK and continue to hire for critical roles and important projects aligned with our company priorities to ensure we can deliver on the opportunity that AI offers the UK economy," they added.

The report said the exit package size for employees in the UK would depend on how long they worked for Google. It was also unclear to which UK organizations the offers went.

data.title: EU expected to approve Omnicom's purchase of Interpublic on Monday - report
data.description:
The European Commission is expected to approve Omnicom's (OMC) $13 billion acquisition of Interpublic (IPG) on Monday.

The European antitrust regulator is set to give unconditional approval for the acquisition, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum reported that was circulating on Thursday. 

Omnicom (OMC) last month said it expects the Interpublic (IPG) deal to close in late November.

The deal received approval from the UK's antitrust regulator  in August.

In December, the companies announced that they would merge in a stock-for-stock transaction valued at $13.3 billion, creating the world’s largest advertising company.

data.title: Ross Stores GAAP EPS of $1.58 beats by $0.16, revenue of $5.6B beats by $190M
data.description:
• Ross Stores press release (ROST): FQ3 GAAP EPS of $1.58 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $5.6B (+9.8% Y/Y) beats by $190M.
  • Shares +3%.

  • Fiscal 2025 Guidance

    Looking ahead, Mr. Conroy said, “We enter the holiday season with strong momentum and are well-positioned to offer a compelling merchandise assortment across all our stores. As a result, for the 13 weeks ending January 31, 2026, we are raising our comparable store sales forecast to be up 3% to 4% with earnings per share in the range of $1.77 to $1.85. This updated guidance range reflects approximately $0.03 earnings per share of unfavorable timing of packaway-related expenses that benefited the third quarter. In addition, we now expect tariff-related costs to be negligible in the fourth quarter.”

    Mr. Conroy continued, “Based on our year-to-date results and updated fourth quarter forecast, we are increasing our earnings per share guidance for fiscal 2025 to be in the range of $6.38 to $6.46, which includes approximately $0.16 per share negative impact from tariff-related costs. In addition, and as a reminder, 2024 fourth quarter and full year earnings per share of $1.79 and $6.32, respectively, included an approximate $0.14 earnings per share benefit related to the sale of a packaway facility.”

    Mr. Conroy concluded, “We are optimistic about our prospects for the holiday season, driven by our ongoing focus on delivering quality, branded merchandise at exceptional value. This approach continues to gain strong traction with the consumer, particularly in an environment of rising prices across mainstream retail. Additionally, the store and supply chain teams are well-positioned for the holiday season, and our new marketing campaigns have continued to build excitement. We believe that this multi-faceted approach will help us continue our positive momentum and enable us to capture additional market share.”

data.title: Kalshi raises $1B in new funding round, valuing prediction market at $11B - report
data.description:
Kalshi (KALSHI) has raised $1B in a new fundraise, valuing the prediction market startup at $11B, according to a media report on Thursday.

The huge round follows a Bloomberg report last month that recent funding offers from venture capital investors would value Kalshi (KALSHI) at $10B-$12B, at least double the $5B valuation set just weeks before.

The company's returning investors, including Sequoia and CapitalG, led the latest round, TechCrunch reported, citing a person familiar. Andreessen Horowitz, Paradigm, Anthos Capital and Neo are among the other investors in Kalshi.

Kalshi (KALSHI) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

In October, rival prediction market Polymarket (POLYMARKET) was reportedly targeting a $15B valuation in a new round of talks with investors.

data.title: SEC dismisses civil action against SolarWinds, security officer
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filed to dismiss its civil enforcement action against SolarWinds and Timothy G. Brown, the company's chief information security officer, the SEC said on Thursday.

Recall that in 2020, SolarWinds was the victim of a massive data breach, which allowed hackers to gain access to data from thousands of organizations, including numerous government agencies, for months. The company was acquired by Turn/River Capital for ~$4.4B earlier this year.

The SEC's decision to seek the dismissal is "in the exercise of its discretion," and "does not necessarily reflect the Commission’s position on any other case," the agency said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
